name: Lamb with Cashew-Nut Curry (Korma)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the masala, combine cashews, chilies, ginger and cold water in a blender. Blend at high speed for 1 minute.
2. Add cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, garlic, poppy seeds, coriander seeds, and cumin to the blender. Pulse until completely pulverized. Set aside.
3. Place saffron in a small bowl, pour boiling water over it, and soak for at least 10 minutes.
4. Heat ghee in a heavy skillet over moderate heat until a drop of water sputters instantly.
5. Add onions to the skillet, stirring constantly, and fry for 7-8 minutes or until soft and golden brown.
6. Stir in salt and the masala mixture, then add yoghurt. Cook occasionally over moderate heat until ghee lightly films the surface.
7. Add lamb cubes, turning them with a spoon to coat evenly.
8. Squeeze saffron into the skillet and stir it with its soaking liquid.
9. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 20 minutes, turning lamb cubes occasionally.
10. Scatter half of the fresh coriander over the lamb and continue cooking, tightly covered for an additional 10 minutes or until lamb is tender.
11. Serve contents in a heated platter, sprinkling with lemon juice and remaining fresh coriander.
